reasoning: The DGS30 yield recently rose from 4.55% in late October to 4.70% by November 7, 2025. In the short term (1-week horizon for Nov 16), mean reversion and the recent local high suggest a slightly higher chance of the yield dropping below the 4.70% mark. Over the medium-to-long term (1-month to 3-month horizons for Dec 9 and Feb 7), structural pressures such as large fiscal deficits, persistent inflation concerns, and a steepening yield curve provide support for higher long-term yields. Thus, the probability of the yield exceeding 4.70% gradually increases with the forecast horizon, passing 50% for the 3-month outlook.
